title: juniperus virginiana var. prostrata
intro: Juniperus virginiana var. prostrata, also known as creeping juniper, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen shrub native to eastern North America, characterized by its woody stems and scale-like leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and diuretic properties, which have been found to help alleviate urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and promote digestion. The health benefits of creeping juniper include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce blood sugar levels, and act as an antioxidant. Creeping juniper is typically prepared for medicinal use through teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any health condition.
